trigger_reason: R756 is rank by mechanical-PASS proximity in E31 (post-filter architectural candidate with kw=2; tree-stream max sim=0.40). Per program_v10.md §2.5 (preserved in v11/v12), top-3 rounds receive full step-13 spec.

A_candidate.name: SU(2)-equivariant transformation module
A_candidate.description: Insert a new SU(2)-equivariant rotation matrix in each layer; matrix is parameterized by a learnable Lie-algebra element and shared across heads. Forward pass: apply the equivariant transformation to keys before attention.

B_control.name: Same architecture WITHOUT the equivariant transformation
B_control.distinctive_parameter_value: no new module / same architecture without the new pathway

statistical_test.name: paired t-test A vs B + A vs C

distinguishability_pre_check.rationale: The SU(2)-equivariant module introduces a genuinely new computation (rotation in 2D-subspace before attention); empirically, the rotation matrix produces a distinct gradient signature from the baseline. Expected: A has different validation BLEU trajectory from B in first 50K steps.
distinguishability_pre_check.v12_observation: Step 05.5 PASS = architectural-topology-change; candidate has a new learnable module with distinct computational signature from baseline. Distinguishability pre-check leans more toward true/uncertain than v11's typical 'uncertain' default.

notes_for_executor: R756's architectural-topology candidate (post-v12-step-05.5-PASS) has structurally-distinct mechanism from baseline B. The new learnable module adds a distinct computation graph edge.
